message:
feat(fcp-core): extend supply-chain HashAlgorithm with SHA-384 and SHA-512

Adds Sha384 and Sha512 variants alongside the existing Blake3_256 and Sha256
options on the supply-chain HashAlgorithm enum so attestation digests can use
the longer SHA-2 hashes that some certificate / SBOM workflows produce.

Functional changes (crates/fcp-core/src/supply_chain.rs):

- HashAlgorithm gains Sha384 and Sha512, each tagged in serde with the canonical
  prefix string ("sha384" / "sha512") to match the existing as_str() contract.
- digest_is_supported, validate_digest_with_algo, and SBOM component validation
  no longer hard-code 64-char hex; a new digest_hex_len(_for_prefix) lookup
  drives an expected_len that is plumbed through is_lower_hex_len. Blake3-256
  and SHA-256 still require 64 hex chars; SHA-384 requires 96; SHA-512 requires
  128.
- hash_bytes is extended with Sha384/Sha512 arms that call into sha2::Sha384 /
  sha2::Sha512, mirroring the Sha256 arm.
- Error strings and the verification-pipeline detail message no longer claim
  "blake3-256 or sha256 with 64 lowercase hex chars" — they now say "a
  supported digest algorithm with matching lowercase hex length" so the message
  is correct for any of the four algorithms.
- All existing is_lower_hex_64_* unit tests are preserved but now call
  is_lower_hex_len(value, 64) so the helper rename is exercised.

Pinning test (crates/fcp-core/tests/hash_algorithm_serde_tags.rs, new):

- hash_algorithm_json_tags_roundtrip pins the JSON serde tag for every variant
  (blake3-256, sha256, sha384, sha512) and the Display/as_str() contract.
- hash_algorithm_cbor_tags_roundtrip pins the deterministic CBOR encoding of
  the same variants by hex-comparing against frozen byte sequences, so any
  future variant rename or serde retag fails loudly instead of silently
  reshaping on-the-wire representations.

No behavior change for existing Blake3-256 / SHA-256 attestations; the new
variants are additive and gated on the canonical prefix appearing in the digest
string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
